preamble: The shift from a sibilant affricate [ts] to a dental fricative [θ] is a notable feature of the Castilian Spanish sibilant shift.

examples.note: The voiceless dental affricate [ts] (represented by 'ç') simplified and shifted to the dental fricative [θ] in northern and central Spain.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
